subject: Statistics
title: Income and Power Laws
description: A micro-lesson that makes Income and Power Laws usable.

hook.text: The richest 1% hold more wealth than the bottom 50% combined. No bell curve explains this.

buildup.text: Income follows a power law at the top. Small talent differences create enormous pay differences.

discovery.text: The bottom 90% look roughly log-normal. The top 10% follow a completely different pattern.

twist.text: Reporting 'average income' mixes two distributions. The median is $40K while the mean is $65K.

climax.text: Income inequality isn't a bell curve problem — it's a power law problem. Different math entirely.

punchline.text: Income has two distributions. Most stats show one.

quiz.question: Why is 'average income' misleading?
options: Income follows two distributions — a few extreme earners distort the mean
Everyone earns close to the average
